item.title: Your resume never reached a human
item.description:
A Look at Amazon’s AI Hiring Tool and the Ethics Behind It

Over the last year or so, I’ve spent a lot more time (then I’d like to admit) updating my resume, writing cover letters, and applying for jobs. Like many other job seekers, I’ve found myself wondering if a real person ever looked at my application or if it was filtered out before anyone had the chance. As companies continue to use artificial intelligence (AI) to help sort through hundreds or even thousands of applications, that question becomes more relevant than ever. AI has become a valuable tool for employers because it can save time by quickly scanning resumes and identifying candidates who appear to be the best fit. However, when AI starts making decisions that affect people’s careers, it’s important to ask whether those decisions are being made fairly. Can an algorithm be unbiased, or does it simply learn from the people who created it and the data it was given?

item.title: The Privacy Paradox: Logging In to an AnonymousSurvey
item.description:
When “Anonymous” Isn’t Enough

Imagine; it’s the end of the semester and you have really struggled with the course, you feel the teacher has favored certain students, and the coursework didn’t make sense. You log in one day and there is a survey for the class that asks how the course was delivered and did it meet your expectations. You think to yourself, “now is my chance to express my concerns with this course! It says it stays anonymous…….right?” And now you begin to worry, because it says it’s anonymous, but how do I know they’re not lying?

item.title: Emoji Support
item.description:
Emoji can be enabled in a Hugo project in a number of ways. :zap:

Emoji Support

The emojify function can be called directly in templates or Inline Shortcodes.

To enable emoji globally, set enableEmoji to true in your site’s configuration and then you can type emoji shorthand codes directly in content files; e.g.

The Emoji cheat sheet is a useful reference for emoji shorthand codes.

N.B. The above steps enable Unicode Standard emoji characters and sequences in Hugo, however the rendering of these glyphs depends on the browser and the platform. To style the emoji you can either use a third party emoji font or a font stack; e.g.

item.title: Placeholder Text
item.description:
Lorem est tota propiore conpellat pectoribus de pectora summo.

Redit teque digerit hominumque toris verebor lumina non cervice subde tollit usus habet Arctonque, furores quas nec ferunt. Quoque montibus nunc caluere tempus inhospita parcite confusaque translucet patri vestro qui optatis lumine cognoscere flos nubis! Fronde ipsamque patulos Dryopen deorum.

  1. Exierant elisi ambit vivere dedere
  2. Duce pollice
  3. Eris modo
  4. Spargitque ferrea quos palude

Rursus nulli murmur; hastile inridet ut ab gravi sententia! Nomine potitus silentia flumen, sustinet placuit petis in dilapsa erat sunt. Atria tractus malis.

item.title: Rich Content
item.description:
Hugo ships with several Embedded Shortcodes for rich content, along with a Privacy Config and a set of Simple Shortcodes that enable static and no-JS versions of various social media embeds.

X Simple Shortcode

{{< x user="GoHugoIO" id="1315233626070503424" >}}

Vimeo Simple Shortcode

{{< vimeo 146022717 >}}


Youtube Simple Shortcode

{{< youtube w7Ft2ymGmfc >}}


Theme Custom Shortcodes

These shortcodes are not Hugo built-ins, but are provided by the theme.
